heading: Administrative review of determinations
label: 1675 Administrative review of determinations

Amendments2016—Subsec. (a)(2)(B)(iii), (iv). added cl. (iv), redesignated former cl. (iv) as (iii), and struck out former cl. (iii). Prior to amendment, text of cl. (iii) read as follows: “The administering authority shall, at the time a review under this subparagraph is initiated, direct the Customs Service to allow, at the option of the importer, the posting, until the completion of the review, of a bond or security in lieu of a cash deposit for each entry of the subject merchandise.”Pub. L. 103–465, § 283(c), amended section generally, substituting present provisions for provisions relating to administrative review of determinations, which provided for periodic review of amount of duty in subsec. (a), review upon information or request in subsec. (b), revocation of countervailing duty order or antidumping duty order in subsec. (c), hearings in subsec. (d), determination that basis for suspension no longer existed in subsec. (e), and correction of ministerial errors in subsec. (f).Pub. L. 103–465, § 220(a)1988—Subsec. (f). added subsec. (f).Pub. children.heading: Periodic review of amount of duty
children.label: a Periodic review of amount of duty
A
children.text: review and determine the amount of any net countervailable subsidy,

children.label: B
children.text: review, and determine (in accordance with paragraph (2)), the amount of any antidumping duty, and

children.label: C
children.text: review the current status of, and compliance with, any agreement by reason of which an investigation was suspended, and review the amount of any net countervailable subsidy or dumping margin involved in the agreement,

children.label: i
children.text: the normal value and export price (or constructed export price) of each entry of the subject merchandise, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the dumping margin for each such entry.

children.heading: Determination of antidumping or countervailing duties for new exporters and producers
children.label: B Determination of antidumping or countervailing duties for new exporters and producers
I
children.text: such exporter or producer did not export the merchandise that was the subject of an antidumping duty or countervailing duty order to the United States (or, in the case of a regional industry, did not export the subject merchandise for sale in the region concerned) during the period of investigation, and

children.label: II
children.text: such exporter or producer is not affiliated (within the meaning of ) with any exporter or producer who exported the subject merchandise to the United States (or in the case of a regional industry, who exported the subject merchandise for sale in the region concerned) during that period,section 1677(33) of this title

children.heading: Time for review under clause (i)
children.label: ii Time for review under clause (i)
I
children.text: the end of the 6-month period beginning on the date of the countervailing duty or antidumping duty order under review, or

children.label: II
children.text: the end of any 6-month period occurring thereafter,

children.heading: Time limits
children.label: iii Time limits
children.text: The administering authority shall make a preliminary determination in a review conducted under this subparagraph within 180 days after the date on which the review is initiated, and a final determination within 90 days after the date the preliminary determination is issued, except that if the administering authority concludes that the case is extraordinarily complicated, it may extend the 180-day period to 300 days and may extend the 90-day period to 150 days.

children.heading: Determinations based on bona fide sales
children.label: iv Determinations based on bona fide sales
I
children.text: the prices of such sales;

children.label: II
children.text: whether such sales were made in commercial quantities;

children.label: III
children.text: the timing of such sales;

children.label: IV
children.text: the expenses arising from such sales;

children.label: V
children.text: whether the subject merchandise involved in such sales was resold in the United States at a profit;

children.label: VI
children.text: whether such sales were made on an arms-length basis; and

children.label: VII
children.text: any other factor the administering authority determines to be relevant as to whether such sales are, or are not, likely to be typical of those the exporter or producer will make after completion of the review.

children.heading: Results of determinations
children.label: C Results of determinations
children.text: The determination under this paragraph shall be the basis for the assessment of countervailing or antidumping duties on entries of merchandise covered by the determination and for deposits of estimated duties.

children.heading: Preliminary and final determinations
children.label: A Preliminary and final determinations
children.text: The administering authority shall make a preliminary determination under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of paragraph (1) within 245 days after the last day of the month in which occurs the anniversary of the date of publication of the order, finding, or suspension agreement for which the review under paragraph (1) is requested, and a final determination under paragraph (1) within 120 days after the date on which the preliminary determination is published. If it is not practicable to complete the review within the foregoing time, the administering authority may extend that 245-day period to 365 days and may extend that 120-day period to 180 days. The administering authority may extend the time for making a final determination without extending the time for making a preliminary determination, if such final determination is made not later than 300 days after the date on which the preliminary determination is published.

children.heading: Liquidation of entries
children.label: B Liquidation of entries
children.text: If the administering authority orders any liquidation of entries pursuant to a review under paragraph (1), such liquidation shall be made promptly and, to the greatest extent practicable, within 90 days after the instructions to Customs are issued. In any case in which liquidation has not occurred within that 90-day period, the Secretary of the Treasury shall, upon the request of the affected party, provide an explanation thereof.

children.heading: Effect of pending review under section 1516a
children.label: C Effect of pending review under section 1516a
children.text: In a case in which a final determination under paragraph (1) is under review under and a liquidation of entries covered by the determination is enjoined under or suspended under , the administering authority shall, within 10 days after the final disposition of the review under , transmit to the Federal Register for publication the final disposition and issue instructions to the Customs Service with respect to the liquidation of entries pursuant to the review. In such a case, the 90-day period referred to in subparagraph (B) shall begin on the day on which the administering authority issues such instructions.section 1516a of this titlesection 1516a(c)(2) of this titlesection 1516a(g)(5)(C) of this titlesection 1516a of this title

children.heading: Absorption of antidumping duties
children.label: 4 Absorption of antidumping duties
children.text: During any review under this subsection initiated 2 years or 4 years after the publication of an antidumping duty order under , the administering authority, if requested, shall determine whether antidumping duties have been absorbed by a foreign producer or exporter subject to the order if the subject merchandise is sold in the United States through an importer who is affiliated with such foreign producer or exporter. The administering authority shall notify the Commission of its findings regarding such duty absorption for the Commission to consider in conducting a review under subsection (c).section 1673e(a) of this title

children.heading: Reviews based on changed circumstances
children.label: b Reviews based on changed circumstances
A
children.text: a final affirmative determination that resulted in an antidumping duty order under this subtitle or a finding under the Antidumping Act, 1921, or in a countervailing duty order under this subtitle or section 1303 of this title,1

children.label: B
children.text: a suspension agreement accepted under section 1671c or 1673c of this title, or

children.label: C
children.text: a final affirmative determination resulting from an investigation continued pursuant to section 1671c(g) or 1673c(g) of this title,

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a countervailing duty order or antidumping duty order or finding, determine whether revocation of the order or finding is likely to lead to continuation or recurrence of material injury,

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a determination made pursuant to section 1671c(h)(2) or 1673c(h)(2) of this title, determine whether the suspension agreement continues to eliminate completely the injurious effects of imports of the subject merchandise, and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of an affirmative determination resulting from an investigation continued under section 1671c(g) or 1673c(g) of this title, determine whether termination of the suspended investigation is likely to lead to continuation or recurrence of material injury.

children.label: A
children.text: the party seeking revocation of an order or finding described in paragraph (1)(A) shall have the burden of persuasion with respect to whether there are changed circumstances sufficient to warrant such revocation, and

children.label: B
children.text: the party seeking termination of a suspended investigation or a suspension agreement shall have the burden of persuasion with respect to whether there are changed circumstances sufficient to warrant such termination.

children.label: A
children.text: the Commission may not review a determination made under section 1671d(b) or 1673d(b) of this title, or an investigation suspended under section 1671c or 1673c of this title, and

children.label: B
children.text: the administering authority may not review a determination made under section 1671d(a) or 1673d(a) of this title, or an investigation suspended under section 1671c or 1673c of this title,

children.label: A
children.text: a countervailing duty order (other than a countervailing duty order to which subparagraph (B) applies or which was issued without an affirmative determination of injury by the Commission under section 1303 of this title), an antidumping duty order, or a notice of suspension of an investigation, described in subsection (a)(1),1

children.label: B
children.text: a notice of injury determination under with respect to a countervailing duty order, orsection 1675b of this title

children.label: C
children.text: a determination under this section to continue an order or suspension agreement,

children.label: A
children.text: a statement expressing their willingness to participate in the review by providing information requested by the administering authority and the Commission,

children.label: B
children.text: a statement regarding the likely effects of revocation of the order or termination of the suspended investigation, and

children.label: C
children.text: such other information or industry data as the administering authority or the Commission may specify.

children.heading: No response
children.label: A No response
children.text: If no interested party responds to the notice of initiation under this subsection, the administering authority shall issue a final determination, within 90 days after the initiation of a review, revoking the order or terminating the suspended investigation to which such notice relates. For purposes of this paragraph, an interested party means a party described in section 1677(9)(C), (D), (E), (F), or (G) of this title.

children.heading: Inadequate response
children.label: B Inadequate response
children.text: If interested parties provide inadequate responses to a notice of initiation, the administering authority, within 120 days after the initiation of the review, or the Commission, within 150 days after such initiation, may issue, without further investigation, a final determination based on the facts available, in accordance with .section 1677e of this title

children.heading: Waiver of participation by certain interested parties
children.label: 4 Waiver of participation by certain interested parties

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An interested party described in section 1677(9)(A) or (B) of this title may elect not to participate in a review conducted by the administering authority under this subsection and to participate only in the review conducted by the Commission under this subsection.

children.heading: Effect of waiver
children.label: B Effect of waiver
children.text: In a review in which an interested party waives its participation pursuant to this paragraph, the administering authority shall conclude that revocation of the order or termination of the investigation would be likely to lead to continuation or recurrence of dumping or a countervailable subsidy (as the case may be) with respect to that interested party.

children.heading: Time limits for completion of review
children.label: A Time limits for completion of review
children.text: Unless the review has been completed pursuant to paragraph (3) or paragraph (4) applies, the administering authority shall make its final determination pursuant to section 1675a(b) or (c) of this title within 240 days after the date on which a review is initiated under this subsection. If the administering authority makes a final affirmative determination, the Commission shall make its final determination pursuant to within 360 days after the date on which a review is initiated under this subsection.section 1675a(a) of this title

children.heading: Extension of time limit
children.label: B Extension of time limit
children.text: The administering authority or the Commission (as the case may be) may extend the period of time for making their respective determinations under this subsection by not more than 90 days, if the administering authority or the Commission (as the case may be) determines that the review is extraordinarily complicated. In a review in which the administering authority extends the time for making a final determination, but the Commission does not extend the time for making a determination, the Commission’s determination shall be made not later than 120 days after the date on which the final determination of the administering authority is published.

children.label: i
children.text: there is a large number of issues,

children.label: ii
children.text: the issues to be considered are complex,

children.label: iii
children.text: there is a large number of firms involved,

children.label: iv
children.text: the orders or suspended investigations have been grouped as described in subparagraph (D), or

children.label: v
children.text: it is a review of a transition order.

children.heading: Grouped reviews
children.label: D Grouped reviews
children.text: The Commission, in consultation with the administering authority, may group orders or suspended investigations for review if it considers that such grouping is appropriate and will promote administrative efficiency. Where orders or suspended investigations have been grouped, the Commission shall, subject to subparagraph (B), make its final determination under this subsection not later than 120 days after the date that the administering authority publishes notice of its final determination with respect to the last order or agreement in the group.

children.heading: Schedule for reviews of transition orders
children.label: A Schedule for reviews of transition orders

children.heading: Initiation
children.label: i Initiation
children.text: The administering authority shall begin its review of transition orders in the 42d calendar month after the date such orders are issued. A review of all transition orders shall be initiated not later than the 5th anniversary after the date such orders are issued.

children.heading: Completion
children.label: ii Completion
children.text: A review of a transition order shall be completed not later than 18 months after the date such review is initiated. Reviews of all transition orders shall be completed not later than 18 months after the 5th anniversary of the date such orders are issued.

children.heading: Subsequent reviews
children.label: iii Subsequent reviews
children.text: The time limits set forth in clauses (i) and (ii) shall be applied to all subsequent 5-year reviews of transition orders by substituting “date of the determination to continue such orders” for “date such orders are issued”.

children.heading: Revocation and termination
children.label: iv Revocation and termination
children.text: No transition order may be revoked under this subsection before the date that is 5 years after the date the WTO Agreement enters into force with respect to the United States.

children.heading: Sequence of transition reviews
children.label: B Sequence of transition reviews
children.text: The administering authority, in consultation with the Commission, shall determine such sequence of review of transition orders as it deems appropriate to promote administrative efficiency. To the extent practicable, older orders shall be reviewed first.

children.label: i
children.text: a countervailing duty order under this subtitle or under section 1303 of this title,1

children.label: ii
children.text: an antidumping duty order under this subtitle or a finding under the Antidumping Act, 1921, or

children.label: iii
children.text: a suspension of an investigation under section 1671c or 1673c of this title,

children.heading: Issue date for transition orders
children.label: D Issue date for transition orders
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a transition order shall be treated as issued on the date the WTO Agreement enters into force with respect to the United States, if such order is based on an investigation conducted by both the administering authority and the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), there shall be excluded from the computation of the 5-year period described in paragraph (1) and the periods described in paragraph (6) any period during which the importation of the subject merchandise is prohibited on account of the imposition, under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act [ et seq.] or other provision of law, of sanctions by the United States against the country in which the subject merchandise originates.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Application of exclusion
children.label: B Application of exclusion
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall apply only with respect to subject merchandise which originates in a country that is not a WTO member.

children.heading: Revocation of order or finding; termination of suspended investigation
children.label: d Revocation of order or finding; termination of suspended investigation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The administering authority may revoke, in whole or in part, a countervailing duty order or an antidumping duty order or finding, or terminate a suspended investigation, after review under subsection (a) or (b). The administering authority shall not revoke, in whole or in part, a countervailing duty order or terminate a suspended investigation on the basis of any export taxes, duties, or other charges levied on the export of the subject merchandise to the United States which are specifically intended to offset the countervailable subsidy received.

children.label: A
children.text: the administering authority makes a determination that dumping or a countervailable subsidy, as the case may be, would be likely to continue or recur, and

children.label: B
children.text: the Commission makes a determination that material injury would be likely to continue or recur as described in .section 1675a(a) of this title

children.heading: Application of revocation or termination
children.label: 3 Application of revocation or termination
children.text: A determination under this section to revoke an order or finding or terminate a suspended investigation shall apply with respect to unliquidated entries of the subject merchandise which are entered, or withdrawn from warehouse, for consumption on or after the date determined by the administering authority.

children.heading: Hearings
children.label: e Hearings
children.text: Whenever the administering authority or the Commission conducts a review under this section, it shall, upon the request of an interested party, hold a hearing in accordance with in connection with that review.section 1677c(b) of this title

children.heading: Determination that basis for suspension no longer exists
children.label: f Determination that basis for suspension no longer exists
children.text: If the determination of the Commission under subsection (b)(2)(B) is negative, the suspension agreement shall be treated as not accepted, beginning on the date of publication of the Commission’s determination, and the administering authority and the Commission shall proceed, under section 1671c(i) or 1673c(i) of this title, as if the suspension agreement had been violated on that date, except that no duty under any order subsequently issued shall be assessed on merchandise entered, or withdrawn from warehouse, for consumption before that date.

children.heading: Reviews to implement results of subsidies enforcement proceeding
children.label: g Reviews to implement results of subsidies enforcement proceeding

children.heading: Violations of article 8 of the subsidies agreement
children.label: 1 Violations of article 8 of the subsidies agreement
A
children.text: the administering authority receives notice from the Trade Representative of a violation of Article 8 of the Subsidies Agreement,

children.label: B
children.text: the administering authority has reason to believe that merchandise subject to an existing countervailing duty order or suspended investigation is benefiting from the subsidy or subsidy program found to have been in violation of Article 8 of the Subsidies Agreement, and

children.label: C
children.text: no review pursuant to subsection (a)(1) is in progress,

children.heading: Withdrawal of subsidy or imposition of countermeasures
children.label: 2 Withdrawal of subsidy or imposition of countermeasures
i
children.text: the United States has imposed countermeasures, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such countermeasures are based on the effects in the United States of imports of merchandise that is the subject of a countervailing duty order, or

children.label: B
children.text: a WTO member country has withdrawn a countervailable subsidy provided with respect to merchandise subject to a countervailing duty order,

children.heading: Expedited review
children.label: 3 Expedited review
children.text: The administering authority shall conduct reviews under this subsection on an expedited basis, and shall publish the results of such reviews in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Correction of ministerial errors
children.label: h Correction of ministerial errors
children.text: The administering authority shall establish procedures for the correction of ministerial errors in final determinations within a reasonable time after the determinations are issued under this section. Such procedures shall ensure opportunity for interested parties to present their views regarding any such errors. As used in this subsection, the term “ministerial error” includes errors in addition, subtraction, or other arithmetic function, clerical errors resulting from inaccurate copying, duplication, or the like, and any other type of unintentional error which the administering authority considers ministerial.
